title: toxicodendron delavayi var. angustifolium
intro: Toxicodendron delavayi var. angustifolium, also known as the narrow-leaved poison oak, is a variety of the poison oak plant, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. The plant contains urushiol, an oil that can cause severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, or tinctures, which are said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and itching.
